title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud infrastructure, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruptions, financial losses, and reputational damage. The use of compromised CI/CD pipelines increases the risk of widespread and rapid deployment of the ransomware.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing multi-factor authentication (MFA), restricting access, and monitoring for unauthorized changes.
Perform regular backups of critical data and ensure backups are stored offline or in a secure, isolated environment.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to monitor for and mitigate ransomware activity in real-time.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal direct risk, but its widespread use could lead to potential exploitation if the tool contains flaws or is used improperly. Attackers might also analyze its behavior to bypass its detection mechanisms.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and functionality before integrating it into your environment to ensure it aligns with your security policies.
Monitor the tool's updates and community feedback to stay informed about any reported vulnerabilities or misuse cases.
Train your team on secure usage practices for open-source tools to minimize risks associated with improper implementation.

title: Open WebUI's Mass Assignment via Pydantic extra='allow' Allows Creating Folders in Other Users' Accounts
description:
# Mass Assignment via Pydantic extra='allow' Allows Creating Folders in Other Users' Accounts

## Affected Component

Folder creation endpoint and form model:
- `backend/open_webui/models/folders.py` (lines 72-77, `FolderForm` with `extra='allow'`)
- `backend/open_webui/models/folders.py` (lines 95-106, `insert_new_folder` dict construction)
- `backend/open_webui/routers/folders.py` (line 119, `create_folder` endpoint)

## Affected Versions

Current main branch (commit `6fdd19bf1`) and likely al

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44550.

title: Open WebUI has an LDAP Empty Password Authentication Bypass
description:
# LDAP Empty Password Authentication Bypass

## Affected Component

LDAP authentication endpoint:
- `backend/open_webui/routers/auths.py` (lines 468-477, user bind with empty password)
- `backend/open_webui/models/auths.py` (lines 58-60, `LdapForm` model)

## Affected Versions

Current main branch (commit `6fdd19bf1`) and likely all versions with LDAP authentication support.

## Description

The LDAP authentication endpoint does not validate that the submitted password is non-empty before perfor

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44551.

title: Grav: Stored XSS via page title (data[header][title]) in admin panel
description:
### Summary
_A Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability was identified in the /admin/pages/[page] endpoint of the Grav application. This vulnerability allows attackers to inject malicious scripts into the data[header][title] parameter._

--- 

### Details
Vulnerable Endpoint: GET /admin/pages/[page]
Parameter: data[header][title]

The application fails to properly validate and sanitize user input in the data[header][title] parameter. As a result, attackers can craft a malicious URL with a

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44737.

title: MikroORM has SQL injection via runtime-controlled identifiers and JSON-path keys
description:
## Summary

MikroORM's identifier-quoting helper (`Platform.quoteIdentifier` and the postgres/mssql overrides) and its JSON-path emitters (`Platform.getSearchJsonPropertyKey`, `quoteJsonKey`) did not properly escape characters that delimit the SQL identifier or string-literal context they emit into. When application code passes attacker-influenced strings to public ORM APIs that expect an identifier or a JSON-property filter, an attacker can break out of the quoted context and inject arbitrary S

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44680.

title: fast-uri vulnerable to host confusion via percent-encoded authority delimiters
description:
### Impact

`fast-uri` v3.1.1 and earlier decodes percent-encoded authority delimiters (`%40` as `@`, `%3A` as `:`) inside the host component and serializes them back as raw characters. This changes the URI structure, turning a hostname into userinfo plus a different host.

For example, `http://trusted.com%40evil.com/` normalizes to `http://trusted.com@evil.com/`, which reparses as host `evil.com` with userinfo `trusted.com`.

Applications that normalize untrusted URLs before host allowlist chec

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6322.

title: Zebra has Permanent Block Discovery Halt via Gossip Queue Saturation and Syncer Poisoning
description:
## Summary

A composite denial-of-service vulnerability in Zebra's block discovery pipeline allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to permanently halt all new block discovery on a targeted node. The attack exploits three independent weaknesses in the gossip, syncer, and download subsystems — all exercisable from a single TCP connection — to create a monotonically growing block deficit that never self-heals.

## Severity

**Critical** — This is a Denial of Service vulnerability that requires n

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to permanently halt block discovery on targeted Zebra nodes, leading to a denial of service. This could disrupt blockchain operations, prevent transaction validation, and compromise network reliability.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply patches or updates provided by the Zebra development team to address CVE-2026-44499.
Implement network-level filtering to restrict unauthorized or suspicious TCP connections to Zebra nodes.
Monitor network traffic for signs of exploitation, such as unusual block deficits or gossip queue saturation, and isolate affected nodes.

title: Bunsink has an SSRF bypass in `validate_webhook_url`
description:
## Summary

Bugsink’s webhook URL validation in versions 2.1.2 and earlier could be (partially) bypassed because of a mismatch in URL parsing.

In some malformed URLs, Python’s standard URL  parser (urllib) and the HTTP client stack (requests / urllib3) do not agree on which host is actually being targeted. That could allow a webhook URL to pass Bugsink’s outbound-host checks while the actual HTTP request is sent somewhere else.

## Impact

This issue affects Bugsink’s outbound webhook integrati

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The SSRF bypass vulnerability in Bugsink's `validate_webhook_url` function could allow attackers to send malicious requests to internal or unauthorized endpoints, potentially leading to data exfiltration, unauthorized access, or service disruption. This could be exploited to bypass security controls and target internal systems.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to the latest version of Bugsink (above 2.1.2) where the vulnerability is patched.
Implement additional server-side validation to ensure webhook URLs are restricted to trusted domains and IP ranges.
Monitor and log webhook requests for unusual or unauthorized activity to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: SiYuan: Electron Renderer RCE via decodeURIComponent-driven tooltip XSS in aria-label sink (incomplete fix for CVE-2026-34585)
description:
## Summary

The tooltip mouseover handler in `app/src/block/popover.ts` reads `aria-label` via `getAttribute` and passes it through `decodeURIComponent` before assigning to `messageElement.innerHTML` in `app/src/dialog/tooltip.ts:41`. The encoder used at the producer side, `escapeAriaLabel` in `app/src/util/escape.ts:19-25`, only handles HTML special characters (`"`, `'`, `<`, literal `<`) — it leaves `%XX` URL-escapes untouched. So a doc title containing `%3Cimg src=x onerror=...%3E` round-t

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44588.

That bypass was closed in v0.4.0 by removing the video exemption from the sanitizer.

The vulnerability described in this advisory is structurally distinct: a markdown-

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-44721.

title: Electerm users can run dangrous code through link or command line
description:
### Impact
_Arbitrary local code execution via deep links, CLI `--opts`, or crafted shortcuts. Affected users: electerm installs that accept protocol URLs or CLI options (affected versions listed in the original report). Exploit requires clicking a crafted `electerm://...` link or opening a crafted shortcut/command that launches electerm with attacker-controlled `opts`._

### Patches
Fixed in version > 3.8.8

Commits:

- https://github.com/electerm/electerm/commit/8a6a17951e96d715f5a231532bbd830

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-43944.

title: Electerm Security Vulnerability: RCE via malicious SSH server filename in openFileWithEditor
description:
### Impact

A code execution (RCE) vulnerability exists in electerm's SFTP open with system editor or "Edit with custom editor" feature. When a user opts to edit a file using open with system editor or open with a custom editor, the filename is passed directly into a command line without sanitization.

A malicious actor controlling the SSH server or user OS can exploit this by crafting a filename containing shell metacharacters. If a victim subsequently attempts to edit this file, the injected c

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote code execution (RCE) via a maliciously crafted filename on an SSH server. Exploitation could lead to arbitrary command execution on the victim's machine, potentially compromising sensitive data, installing malware, or gaining unauthorized access to systems.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest patched version of Electerm that addresses CVE-2026-43943, or apply any available security patches immediately.
Avoid connecting to untrusted SSH servers and verify the integrity of filenames before using the 'open with system editor' or 'edit with custom editor' features.
Implement strict input sanitization and validation for filenames in custom workflows to prevent shell metacharacter injection.

title: Electerm's full process.env exposed to renderer via window.pre.env
description:
### Impact

The `getConstants()` IPC handler in `src/app/lib/ipc-sync.js` serialises the entire `process.env` object and sends it to the renderer. The data is stored as `window.pre.env` and is accessible from any JavaScript running in the renderer (e.g., via the DevTools console or a compromised webview context).

On developer and CI machines, `process.env` routinely contains secrets such as:

- `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` / `AWS_SESSION_TOKEN`
- `GITHUB_TOKEN` / `NPM_TOKEN`
- `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `DO

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-43942.

title: Electerm has an unvalidated shell.openExternal that allows arbitrary protocol execution via terminal link click
description:
### Impact

Electerm's terminal hyperlink handler passes any URL clicked in the terminal directly to `shell.openExternal` without any protocol validation.

When a user connects to a malicious SSH server, the attacker can print a crafted URI in the terminal output. If the victim clicks the link, `shell.openExternal` executes it using the operating system's default protocol handler.

This can be abused to:
- Trigger dangerous protocol handlers (`ms-msdt:`, `search-ms:`) for code execution
- Open l

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Electerm allows attackers to exploit unvalidated terminal link handling, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution or unauthorized actions via dangerous protocol handlers. This poses a significant risk to system integrity and user data.
recommended_actions: Update Electerm to a patched version as soon as a fix is released to address the vulnerability.
Implement strict user training to avoid clicking on suspicious or unknown links in terminal outputs.
Use network segmentation and endpoint protection to limit exposure to malicious SSH servers and mitigate potential damage.

title: Electerm runWidget has a path traversal that leads to arbitrary code execution
description:
### Impact
The `runWidget` function in `src/app/widgets/load-widget.js` constructs a file path by directly concatenating user‑supplied widget identifiers without any sanitisation:

```javascript
const file = `widget-${widgetId}.js`
const widget = require(path.join(__dirname, file))
```

Because `runWidget` is exposed to the renderer process via an asynchronous IPC handler with no input validation, an attacker who achieves JavaScript execution inside the renderer (for example, through a malicious

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-43940.

title:
Crypt::PasswdMD5 versions through 1.42 for Perl generates insecure random values for salts.

The...

Crypt::PasswdMD5 versions through 1.42 for Perl generates insecure random values for salts.

The built-in rand function is predictable, and unsuitable for cryptography.

title: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (XSS) vulnerability in absinthe...
description:
Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (XSS) vulnerability in absinthe-graphql absinthe_plug allows reflected cross-site scripting via the GraphiQL interface.

'Elixir.Absinthe.Plug.GraphiQL':js_escape/1 in lib/absinthe/plug/graphiql.ex escapes single quotes and newlines in the query GET parameter before embedding it in an inline JavaScript string, but does not escape backslashes. An attacker can bypass the escaping by prefixing a quote with a backslash (e.g. \'), breaking o

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks via the GraphiQL interface, potentially leading to the execution of malicious scripts in the context of the user's browser. This could result in data theft, session hijacking, or unauthorized actions on behalf of the user.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to the latest version of absinthe-graphql absinthe_plug where the vulnerability is patched.
Implement additional input validation and sanitization mechanisms to mitigate XSS risks.
Restrict access to the GraphiQL interface to trusted users or environments only.

title: Directory Traversal vulnerability in fohrloop dash-uploader v.0.1.0 through v.0.7.0a2 allows a...
description: Directory Traversal vulnerability in fohrloop dash-uploader v.0.1.0 through v.0.7.0a2 allows a remote attacker to execute arbitrary code via the dash_uploader/httprequesthandler.py, aseHttpRequestHandler.get_temp_root(), BaseHttpRequestHandler._post() components

recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to a patched version of fohrloop dash-uploader if available, or apply mitigations such as restricting access to the vulnerable components.
Implement input validation and sanitization to prevent exploitation of directory traversal vulnerabilities.
Monitor systems for unusual activity and review logs for signs of exploitation attempts targeting the affected components.

title: Akamai Guardicore Platform Agent (GPA) and Zero Trust Client on Linux and macOS allow TOCTOU...
description: Akamai Guardicore Platform Agent (GPA) and Zero Trust Client on Linux and macOS allow TOCTOU-based local privilege escalation. The GPA service creates an IPC socket in the world-writable /tmp directory. It accepts unauthenticated IPC control messages. This enables a TOCTOU vulnerability in the HandleSaveLogs() function of the GPA service, by creating a log file and manipulating it into a symlink that points to the targeted path; this can allow an unprivileged local user to make arbitrary root-ow

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-34354.

title: An issue was discovered in kosma minmea 0.3.0. The minmea_scan functions format specifier copies...
description: An issue was discovered in kosma minmea 0.3.0. The minmea_scan functions format specifier copies NMEA field data to a caller-provided buffer without a size parameter. Applications using minmea_scan on untrusted input are vulnerable to a stack buffer overflow.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-29974.

title: nanoMODBUS through v1.22.0 has a stack-based buffer overflow in recv_read_registers_res() in...
description: nanoMODBUS through v1.22.0 has a stack-based buffer overflow in recv_read_registers_res() in nanomodbus.c. When a client calls nmbs_read_holding_registers() or nmbs_read_input_registers(), the library writes register data from the server response to the caller-provided buffer based on the response's byte_count field before validating that byte_count matches the requested quantity. A malicious Modbus TCP server can send a response with byte_count=250 (125 registers) regardless of the requested qu

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-29972.

title: CVE-2026-7548 - A vulnerability was detected in Totolink NR1800X 9.1.0u.6279_B20210910. This affects the function su
description: A vulnerability was detected in Totolink NR1800X 9.1.0u.6279_B20210910. This affects the function sub_41A68C of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi. Performing a manipulation of the argument setUssd results in command injection. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Totolink NR1800X allows remote command injection via the setUssd argument in the cgi-bin interface. This could enable attackers to execute arbitrary commands on the device, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or control over the network.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any available firmware updates or patches from Totolink to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict remote access to the device by configuring firewall rules and disabling unnecessary remote management interfaces.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and consider isolating affected devices from critical systems until the vulnerability is resolved.

description: A security vulnerability has been detected in Totolink NR1800X 9.1.0u.6279_B20210910. The impacted element is the function find_host_ip of the component lighttpd. title: CVE-2026-7545 - A weakness has been identified in SourceCodester Advanced School Management System 1.0. The affected
description: A weakness has been identified in SourceCodester Advanced School Management System 1.0. The affected element is an unknown function of the file commonController.php of the component checkEmail Endpoint. This manipulation causes sql injection. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The identified SQL injection vulnerability in SourceCodester Advanced School Management System 1.0 could allow attackers to manipulate database queries, potentially leading to unauthorized data access, data exfiltration, or complete database compromise. Since the exploit is publicly available, the risk of exploitation is significantly heightened.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input sanitization and parameterized queries to mitigate SQL injection vulnerabilities in the affected component.
Update or patch the SourceCodester Advanced School Management System to the latest version if a fix is available.
Monitor logs and implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious SQL injection attempts.

title: CVE-2026-22726 - Route Services can be leveraged to send app traffic to network destinations outside of an app's conf
description:
Route Services can be leveraged to send app traffic to network destinations outside of an app's configured egress rules. As a result, a malicious developer with access to Cloudfoundry could configure a route-service that would allow it to send requests to HTTP services on internal networks reachable by the Gorouter, which may not have previously had direct access from outside networks, or from the application.
Routing release: affected from v0.118.0 through v0.371.0 (inclusive); upgrade to v0.372.0 or greater. CF Deployment: affected from v0.0.2 through v54.14.0 (inclusive); upgrade to v55.0.0 or greater (includes routing_release v0.372.0).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-22726.
